mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate improving labor market conditions, which boost consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:At snapshot PAYEMS=131017, trending upward from crisis lows
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC below 0.03 or average forward 20-day return during PAYEMS increases is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains above 8.5%, it signals persistent labor market slack that depresses aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 8.5, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirms strengthening consumer and housing demand, a reliable signal of economic expansion that pushes S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Both measures are cyclically sensitive demand indicators
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS month-over-month growth are positive, the 20-day SPX forward return is negative more than 50% of the time or the mean return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery phase, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong manufacturing revival and business investment, which directly boosts equity valuations over the short term.
failure_condition: During NBER-dated recovery periods, the 5-day SPX forward return following positive INDPRO surprises is not significantly higher than during non-recovery periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and raise expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window between CPIAUCSL percent change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is above -0.03 (i.e., not reliably negative) or average return during rising CPIAUCSL periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 600,000 units monthly signals a sustainable housing market recovery that strengthens consumer wealth and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Snapshot HOUST=560, just below the 600 threshold
failure_condition: In months following HOUST > 600, the 20-day SPX forward return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of positive returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%) and near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%) indicates a liquidity trap where monetary policy loses effectiveness, deepening recession fears and causing short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: When the joint trigger condition holds, the 5-day SPX forward return is positive more than 50% of the time or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During confirmed economic expansions, rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand that directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to higher S&P 500 levels over a multi-month horizon.
failure_condition: During NBER-dated expansions, rolling 12-month Rank IC between RSAFS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.05 or the mean forward return during rising RSAFS periods is not significantly positive.
